data.title: Hang Seng Index Opens 0.5% Lower at 24115.98
PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 7.1733 (vs. estimate at 7.2355)
data.description:
The People's Bank of China (PBOC), China's central bank, is responsible for setting the daily midpoint of the yuan (also known as renminbi or RMB). The PBOC follows a managed floating exchange rate system that allows the value of the yuan to fluctuate within a certain range, called a "band," around a central reference rate, or "midpoint." It's currently at +/- 2%.

The previous close was 7.2430

PBoC injects 96.5bn yuan via 7-Day Reverse Repos at 1.5%

  • 97bn mature today
  • net drains 0.5bn yuan in Open Market Operations

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Opens 0.35% Lower at 6016.34
Bumi Armada Target Price Raised to MYR0.71 From MYR0.69 by CIMB Securities >5210.KU
Shanghai Composite Index Opens Flat at 3373.26
Japan Loan Growth Accelerates in February
data.description:
Japan's loan growth accelerated in February 2025, with the total value of loans rising 3.1% year-on-year, up from a downwardly revised 2.9% increase in January and matching market expectations.

This marked the strongest expansion in seven months.

Outstanding loans held by major, regional, and "shinkin" banks reached 635.4 trillion yen.

Major banks led the growth with a 3.4% increase, followed by regional banks at 2.9%, while "shinkin" banks—which cater to smaller businesses and local communities—saw a modest 0.9% rise.

data.title: Is the chip sector having a meltdown? By this measure, it's mostly just Nvidia.
data.description:
By Emily Bary

Broadcom and Marvell shares have been hit recently. But Nvidia alone is responsible for about three quarters of the chip sector's lost market cap in the weeks since the DeepSeek news.

Many high-profile chip stocks got punished this earnings season, with Wall Street taking a more exacting approach to evaluating artificial-intelligence players.

But when you look at the carnage in the past six weeks since the DeepSeek developments rocked the technology sector, one company is largely responsible for the lost market value. That's Nvidia Corp. (NVDA), which is by far the biggest chip company to begin with.

Through Thursday's close, the PHLX Semiconductor Index SOX had lost $1.04 trillion in market value since Jan. 27, when markets first began to reflect fears about DeepSeek and the possibility that future AI development could require a lot less expensive hardware than previously thought. But about three quarters of that decline, or $783 billion, corresponded to Nvidia, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Shares of Nvidia have lagged the S&P 500 SPX and the PHLX Semiconductor Index since the DeepSeek selloff began. Broadcom Inc.'s stock (AVGO) has seen comparable losses and Marvell Technology Inc.'s stock (MRVL) has seen even bigger ones, but they have smaller market values - especially in the case of Marvell - so they contribute less to the chip index's market-cap decline. Nvidia is valued at $2.76 trillion, compared with $914 billion for Broadcom and $61 billion for Marvell.

Within the "Magnificent Seven" grouping of large technology companies, only Tesla Inc.'s stock (TSLA) has performed worse than Nvidia's over this span.

Nvidia is also about $900 billion off its peak market capitalization achieved on Jan. 6.

See more: Nvidia's stock selloff deepens after Marvell earnings as analyst says "sentiment is rough'

While Nvidia has compelling growth opportunities ahead with its Blackwell offerings and is expected to share even more about its product pipeline at its annual GTC conference later this month, investors seem to be taking issue with various things in the company's financials. For one, the company isn't beating its guidance by the same magnitude that it did earlier in the AI cycle. And the transition to Blackwell is putting pressure on the company's gross margins.

Mizuho desk-based analyst Jordan Klein noted Friday that Broadcom may be seen as "the best of the bunch" in AI semiconductors right now. The company is no Nvidia, but because it's smaller than Nvidia, that creates "runway for much higher AI [revenue] expansion." Broadcom is also seeing good visibility and growing customer traction for its application-specific integrated circuits.

Read: Why Broadcom's earnings are clicking with investors after Nvidia's fell flat

That said, while Broadcom's stock was rewarded after the company's earnings this week, it hasn't done much better than Nvidia's since the DeepSeek news came out. Broadcom shares are down 20.3% while Nvidia shares are off 21%.

Non-AI chip plays have held up better than the S&P 500 and the PHLX Semiconductor Index over that span. While the S&P 500 is off 5.3% and the chip index is off 13.2%, shares of Analog Devices Inc. (ADI) are up 4.4% and shares of NXP Semiconductor NV (NXPI) are up 4.3%.

Within the semiconductor sector, money may be rotating into makers of analog chips that have potentially interesting recovery narratives ahead. "We believe the analog recovery is imminent and that every analog company will feel it as sales from the analog companies have fallen roughly 30% from the peak," Citi Research analyst Christopher Danely wrote last month as he turned bullish on NXP shares in the wake of upbeat market commentary from Analog Devices' management.

There are still challenges in the automotive market, he noted, but trends in the industrial market are getting better.

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Alligator Energy Files Program for Environment Protection, Rehabilitation at South Australia Field Recovery Trial
data.description:
Alligator Energy submitted the program for environment protection and rehabilitation for the in-situ field recovery trial at the Samphire project in South Australia, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

Once approved, the document will form the field recovery trial operating plan, the filing said.

The approval is the final step in the regulatory process for on-site construction of trial infrastructure, while an Environmental Protection Agency site permit process is also underway, per the filing.

data.title: Fiji Inflation Rate Eases from 4-Month High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Fiji slowed to 1.4% in February 2025, down from January’s four-month high of 4.0%, due to a sharp moderation in food prices (1.4% vs 9.3% in January).

Prices also rose more slowly for furnishing, household equipment, and routine maintenance (0.1% vs 0.4%), while the cost of recreation and culture remained flat.

Meanwhile, prices accelerated for alcoholic beverages and tobacco (7.5% vs 7.1%), education (0.5% vs 0.4%), and miscellaneous goods and services (8.6% vs 1.1%).

Additionally, prices continued to fall for housing (-0.4% vs -1.0%), and clothing & footwear (-0.8% vs -0.4%), while transport prices dropped the least in five months (-2.1% vs -3.5%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices declined by 1.3% in February, reversing a 2.0% rise in January, which had been the fastest pace since February 2021.

data.title: Mitsui O.S.K.: Acquisition Expected to Be Completed in June
RailTel Corp. of India Bags INR283 Million Work Order
data.description:
RailTel Corp. of India has received a work order from Northern Railway, India, worth 282.9 million Indian rupees, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The contract entails the provision of indoor and outdoor double distant signaling work to be executed by Sept. 6, 2026.

data.title: Seven & i in talks with Couche-Tard over store sales for merger deal
data.description:
Japan's Seven & i Holdings said on Monday that talks have begun with Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (ACT) over a store sale plan that would set the stage for the latter's $47 billion takeover bid.

Last week, the 7-Eleven convenience store operator named Stephen Dacus as its new CEO to lead a recovery and respond to the takeover offer from Couche-Tard .

Seven & i has said U.S. antitrust law would be a barrier to any deal. The companies are the top two players in the U.S. convenience store market, with about 20,000 locations between them.

In a letter to shareholders on Monday, Seven & i said it proposed that the two companies could map out the viability of a divestiture process and identify potential buyers.

Couche-Tard "recently agreed" to the proposal that would allow for an assessment of the Canadian company's buyout offer, Seven & i said.

Separately, Seven & i said Joseph Michael DePinto stepped down as a director of the holding company while remaining the chief executive of 7-Eleven Inc.

Top executives from Couche-Tard are due to visit Tokyo this week to speak with media about their takeover bid.

Artisan Partners , a U.S.-based investor in Seven & i Holdings, said on Sunday it opposed the Japanese retailer's CEO succession plan and urged the company to reconsider Couche-Tard's takeover offer.

data.title: Nikkei Trades Flat as Consumer, Railway Stocks Gain — Market Talk
data.description: The Nikkei Stock Average is flat at 36886.32, as gains in consumer and railway stocks help offset losses in electronics and machinery stocks. Unicharm rises 4.2% and Seibu Holdings is 2.2% higher, while Kioxia Holdings declines 3.4% and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries is 3.3% lower. USD/JPY is at 147.30, compared with 147.56 as of Friday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to U.S. trade policy as well as Japanese monetary policy. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

2012 ET - The Nikkei Stock Average is flat at 36886.32, as gains in consumer and railway stocks help offset losses in electronics and machinery stocks. Unicharm rises 4.2% and Seibu Holdings is 2.2% higher, while Kioxia Holdings declines 3.4% and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries is 3.3% lower. USD/JPY is at 147.30, compared with 147.56 as of Friday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to U.S. trade policy as well as Japanese monetary policy. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2047 ET - The yen strengthens against most other G-10 and Asian currencies in early trade amid prospects of large wage increases in Japan. The Japanese Trade Union Confederation, known as Rengo, is scheduled to announce preliminary results for the annual spring wage negotiations this Friday. "We expect Rengo to announce another strong wage result on Friday which could bring forward expectations of BOJ rate hikes," CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research team says in a note. U.S.-Japan interest-rate differentials could narrow further and pull down USD/JPY, the team adds. USD/JPY falls 0.4% to 147.30; AUD/JPY sheds 0.4% to 92.97. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2041 ET - South Korea's benchmark Kospi edges up 0.1% to 2566.21 in early mixed trade, reversing from its opening fall. Retail investors are net buyers while foreign and institutional investors are net sellers. Energy stocks advance while shipbuilding shares retreat. Oil refiners SK Innovation and S-Oil rise 4.5% and 5.4%, respectively. Electric-vehicle battery maker LG Energy Solution gains 2.9%. Shipbuilder HD Hyundai Heavy Industries and Hanwha Ocean fall 4.6% and 3.6%, respectively. USD/KRW is 0.1% higher at 1,447.60, compared with Friday's Seoul onshore trading close. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is down 3.3 bps at 2.773%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

2013 ET - JGBs edge lower in the morning Tokyo session, tracking Friday's price declines in U.S. Treasurys. JGBs and Treasurys tend to move in tandem. The domestic market may also be weighed by Japanese data released earlier showing scheduled cash earnings rose 3.1% in January versus 2.6% in December. "Regular pay growth surged in January and with this year's spring wage negotiations set to result in stronger pay hikes than last year, wage growth will pick up further over the course of the year," Capital Economics' Marcel Thieliant says in commentary. "There's an increasingly strong case for a rate hike" at the BOJ's April 30-May 1 meeting, the head of Asia-Pacific says. The JGB 5-year yield is up 1.5 bps at 1.135%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1947 ET - Japanese stocks may rise as concerns about U.S. tariffs and higher borrowing costs ebb for now. Nikkei futures are up 0.5% at 36985 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 147.52, compared with 147.56 as of Friday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to U.S. trade policy as well as Japanese monetary policy. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 2.2% to 36887.17 on Friday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

data.title: Winsome Resources Says Geochemical Data Review From Canada Project Confirms Lithium, Cesium Mineralization Potential
data.description:
Winsome Resources said that a review of geochemical data from its Sirmac-Clapier project in Canada confirmed the presence of systems that form high-grade lithium and cesium mineralization, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The geochemical analysis determined that the pegmatite rock formation at the project is highly fractionated, an indicator of the potential for strong mineralization.

The confirmed cesium grades of more than 1% of cesium oxide suggest pollucite is the dominant cesium-bearing mineral, the filing said. The company is conducting a mineralogical analysis to verify this conclusion.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Sunday at 9 PM ET
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fall after a rough week on Wall Street, as Trump won't rule out recession

U.S. stock futures fell Sunday, as President Donald Trump offered little reassurance after the S&P 500 had its worst week since September.

Trump Declines to Rule Out Recession

In an interview that aired Sunday, the president demurred when asked if there were reassurances he could provide to businesses seeking more clarity on tariffs.

Tariff Wars Are Often Short. Their Legacies Aren't.

Economists fear that Trump's tariffs, once they take hold, could have unexpected effects long after he leaves office.

Markets Finally Woke Up to Tariff Reality. Is This a Buying Opportunity?

Your correspondent remains torn, but if you believe in the Trump pitch, now might be a time to take a swing.

China Struggles to Shake Off Disinflationary Pressure as Trade Threats Loom

Consumer prices nosedived in February, ending a year of tepid price growth.

America's job market is eerily similar to the 1990s dot-com bubble - and, yes, it's a worry

March 20 marks the 25th anniversary of the dot-com bust. Could it happen again?

GOP Government Funding Plan Sets Up Clash With Democrats

The measure to avoid a government shutdown next weekend boosts military funding, cuts nondefense spending.

Stock Investors Go on Defense With Dividends

Some investors are sheltering from market swings in shares of companies with high payouts.

A Government Shutdown Could Create Short-Term Volatility. But It's Mostly Noise.

Tariffs aren't the only concern in Washington DC. A looming government shutdown could temporarily roil the markets too.

How European Defense Stocks Became a Hedge-Fund Home Run

Drastic shifts in the global security environment have sparked a sector rally.

Is the chip sector having a meltdown? By this measure, it's mostly just Nvidia.

data.title: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines: To Buy LBC Tank Terminals Group Holding Netherlands
Market Chatter: South Korea to Unveil Measures Against US Auto Tariffs in April
data.description:
South Korea will put forward measures in April to counter the US' plan to impose tariffs on auto imports, Yonhap News reported Friday, citing the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.

South Korean automakers, including Hyundai Motor and GM Korea, warned that the tariffs could severely impact exports and the local auto parts sector. The carmakers urged the government to highlight their investments in the US and explore support measures such as export diversification and policy financing, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Australian coal miners surge after China logs record coal imports
data.description:

data.title: Mitsui O.S.K.: To Buy LBC Tank Terminals for $1.715B
Hyterra to Start Drilling First Wells at Kansas Project
data.description:
Hyterra will start drilling the first wells of its multi-well exploration program at the Nemaha project in Kansas in April, according to a Monday filing with Australian bourse.

The drilling is part of a 12-month plan to explore the potential of natural (white) hydrogen in Kansas through its subsidiary, HYT Operating, the filing said.

A geophysical program will be conducted to support future well drilling in the second half of the year, starting with airborne surveying this month, per the filing.

data.title: Australia's Avenira rises to near 1-month high as co secures investment from largest shareholder
data.description:
** Shares of Avenira rise 28.6% to A$0.009, their highest levels since Feb 13

** Stock eyes best trading session since late July 2024, if current gains hold

** The specialty miner says it has secured an investment from a unit of China-based Sichuan Hebang Biotechnology , co's largest shareholder

** The investment comprises a A$7.567 mln ($4.77 mln) placement at an issue price of A$0.007, in line with stock's last close

** Sichuan Hebang also agrees to provide unsecured draw-down loan facility

** Funds to be used to advance co's Wonarah Direct Shipping Ore (DSO) phosphate project in the Northern Territory

** Stock up 5.9% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.5853 Australian dollars)

data.title: Market Chatter: Wesfarmers Tests New Retail Brand Atomica
data.description:
Wesfarmers rebranded its Sydney outlet Priceline as Atomica aiming to experiment with mid-range beauty products, the Australian Financial Review reported on Sunday, citing health managing director Emily Amos.

The company intends to open four more such stores by the end of the financial year, after which the company will hold off the rollout and evaluate, the report said.

The company aims to compete with the likes of Mecca and Sephora with moderate pricing, with products ranging from brands like Bubble Skincare, Pixi, Innisfree, and CeraVe, among others, according to the report.

Westfarmers did not immediately respond to a request for comment by MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Delta Lithium Posts Narrower Fiscal H1 Loss, Revenue Flat
data.description:
Delta Lithium reported Monday a fiscal first-half loss of AU$0.0002 per diluted share, narrower compared with a loss of AU$0.0031 a year earlier.

Other income for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$1.3 million, compared with AU$1.3 million a year earlier.

data.title: Gold edges higher on dollar weakness, safe-haven flows
data.description:
Gold prices edged higher on Monday, helped by a weaker dollar and safe-haven flows triggered by fears of a global trade war, while investors awaited further clues to gauge the Federal Reserve's interest rate stance.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold was up 0.1% at $2,914.42 an ounce, as of 0052 GMT, while U.S. gold futures rose 0.3% to $2,921.90.

* U.S. President Donald Trump declined to predict whether the U.S. could face a recession amid stock market concerns about his tariff actions on Mexico, Canada and China over fentanyl.

* Seesaw tariff announcements have unnerved Wall Street as investors say flip-flopping moves by the Trump administration to roll back levies on trading partners are causing confusion rather than bringing relief.

* Trump imposed new 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada last Tuesday, along with fresh duties on Chinese goods.

* He later exempted many imports from Mexico and some from Canada from those tariffs for a month, creating uncertainty in the markets and fanning worries about U.S. inflation and growth.

* Tariffs have been a key concern for investors, with many believing that they can harm economic growth and be inflationary.

* Worries over Trump's tariff policies pushed safe-haven gold to a record high of $2,956.15 on February 24.

* Gold is seen as a hedge against political risks and inflation.

* However, if rising price pressures force the Fed to keep interest rates higher, gold may lose its allure as it is a non-yielding asset.

* The Labor Department's employment report on Friday showed that nonfarm payrolls increased by 151,000 last month after rising by a downwardly revised 125,000 in January.

* Investors now await U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) data on Wednesday and Producer Price Index (PPI) data on Thursday.

* Spot silver firmed 0.1% to $32.56 an ounce, platinum was steady at $962.90, and palladium eased 0.2% to $946.30.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

0700 Germany Industrial Output MM, Industrial Production YY SA MM Jan

data.title: NZX Midday Sector Update: Consumer Non-Durables Soar, Commercial Services Struggle
data.description:
Consumer non-durables stocks gained the most on the New Zealand exchange, rising above 1% midday Friday.

Fonterra Co-operative Group shares rose past 3% in recent Monday trade as it raised its fiscal 2025 earnings guidance to NZ$0.55 to NZ$0.75 per share from a prior range of NZ$0.40 to NZ$0.60 per share.

On the other hand, commercial services shares dropped almost 1%.

Accordant Group shares fell almost 3% in recent trade, reaching an all-time low.
